name: Boreal Agent Production Access Packet Example
description: Example-only packet an external agent can mirror when requesting scoped operator review for live Boreal HTTP use. This packet is not a credential, permission grant, human approval record, payment authorization, completion proof, or production sandbox.

representedActor.authorizationEvidence: The represented supply owner will approve every write-capable Request action through a Boreal account session or operator-confirmed pilot gate.
representedActor.approvalBoundary: The agent may propose Commitments, submit Artifacts, and monitor activity only for Requests where this represented actor is authorized by route policy.

requestedAccess.duration: 30 day low-volume pilot, renewed only after operator review.
requestedAccess.rateLimitPlan: Read public board at bounded intervals, submit at most 5 proposals and 10 artifacts per day, and stop on rate-limit, missing-scope, or blocked agentActionPolicy responses.
requestedAccess.dataAccessJustification: The agent needs public request reads and scoped request-bound write access only where the represented supply owner is eligible to apply or submit proof.
requestedAccess.humanApprovalModel: A represented human reviews every Commitment proposal, proof packet, payment-sensitive escalation, and completion claim before the agent acts or reports finality.

deliveryExpectations: Use signed push only after a live subscription contract exists.
Poll request activity with cursor.nextAfterSequence until signed push delivery is live.
Treat webhook delivery ids as transport ids, not RequestEvent truth.

dataHandling.retention: Store request ids, artifact ids, idempotency keys, cursor checkpoints, and redacted packet summaries for 30 days; do not store raw session cookies or bearer tokens.
dataHandling.redactionPlan: Redact private prompts, credentials, payment details, owner-only chat text, and unrelated personal data before public artifacts or review packets.

idempotencyAndRecoveryPlan.retryStrategy: Retry uncertain writes only with the same idempotency key and identical semantic input after reading current Request, Commitment, Artifact, Transaction, and RequestEvent truth.

paymentAndSpendBoundary.transactionReconciliation: This packet requests no spend authority. Any future paid run, funding, buyer-credit debit, or x402-capable endpoint must reconcile into Transaction truth and require explicit represented-human approval.
forbiddenRequests: payment-agent credential before a live route contract exists

humanEscalation.responseWindow: One business day for low-volume pilot blockers; immediate stop for payment, privacy, or duplicate mutation risk.
blockingQuestions: Which represented human or supply account may the agent act for?
Which Request classes and geographies are in scope for the pilot?
Which proof packets may the agent submit without additional reviewer context?
Who receives urgent stop, revocation, payment, or privacy escalations?

requestedDecision.decisionRequested: Review for approved_scoped_pilot after a real credential path exists; otherwise keep public-read only.
operatorShouldCheck: requested scopes map one-to-one to intended actions
sandbox evidence covers every requested write class
human approval model blocks opening, funding, accepting, spending, and completion claims
idempotency and recovery plan prevents duplicate Commitment, Artifact, Transaction, and RequestEvent writes
protocol claims keep MCP, A2A, OAuth-compatible delegation, signed push, and x402 target-only

rules: A production access packet is review input only; it does not create live authority.
Credential issuance, if any, must happen through a separate live auth path and still obey route scopes, request state, participant role, idempotency, and agentActionPolicy.
Every write, spend, approval, review, and completion claim still needs represented-human approval where the relevant profile requires it.
Target protocol claims need separate adapter contracts before production use.
